original: Scientists announce the discovery of the oldest & most distant galaxy known to man, EGS-zs8-1
16th and final MLB pre-season Mayor's Trophy Game prior to inter-league play; NY Mets beat NY Yankees, 4-1 to hold 8-7-1 edge
Conjunction of Sun, Mercury, Venus, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, and the Moon
The Vatican announces the Popemobile will be donated and converted into a mobile health clinic for wounded children in Gaza, Palestine
Cy Youngpitches the first perfect game in "modern" baseball as the Boston Americans beat Philadelphia Athletics, 3-0
Eugene Bullardgains his pilot's license from Aéro-Club de France and becomes the 1st African-American military pilot (French Air Service)
Alan Shepardbecomes the first American in space aboard Freedom 7
Childish Gambino [Donald Glover] releases the music video to new single "This is America" to wide acclaim
Masterpiece Radio Theater begins broadcasting
Voyager 1 flies by Jupiter
Archaeornithura meemannae, the oldest known prehistoric bird, is discovered
Baltimore Orioles pitcher John Means no-hits Seattle Mariners, 6-0 at T-Mobile Park, Seattle
Amy Johnsontakes off - first woman to fly solo from England to Australia
Canada is the first country to authorize the Pfizer COVID-19 vaccine for 12-15 year olds
Evidence of Africa's earliest burial, a three-year-old boy, 78,000 years ago, in a cave in Kenya published
Huge floods in Brazil's Rio Grande do Sul state reportedly kill 100 people with 100 still missing, some areas record 300 millimeters (11.8 inches) of rain
Music Hall (now Carnegie Hall) opens in New York City, withPyotr Ilyich Tchaikovskyas guest conductor of the New York Music Society Orchestra
Delia Derbyshire, English musician and composer (Doctor Who theme; White Noise), born in Coventry, England (d. 2001)
Stanley Cup Final, Olympia Stadium, Detroit, MI: Montreal Canadiens earn back-to-back titles; beat Detroit Red Wings, 3-2 in OT for a 4-2 series victory
Kublai Khan, grandson of Genghis Khan, becomes ruler of the Mongol Empire
Indian Wars:Sitting Bullleads his band of Lakota into Canada to avoid harassment by the United States Army under Colonel Nelson Miles
The British and Foreign Society for Improving the Embossed Literature of the Blind adopts Braille as best format for blind people
"Lucy Does a TV Commercial" (also known as "Vitameatavegamin") episode of "I Love Lucy" premieres, garnering 68% of US television viewers
Jim Bailey (US) runs mile a record 3:58.6 in LA, California
Milwaukee Bucks sign #1 NBA Draft pick, star UCLA center Lew Alcindor (Kareem Abdul-Jabbar)

National release of "Field of Dreams", a sports fantasy drama film based on W. P. Kinsella's novel "Shoeless Joe", starringKevin Costner,James Earl Jones,Ray LiottaandBurt Lancasterin his final film role
Michael J. Adams, USAF pilot (X-15), born in Sacramento, California (d. 1967)
French ballerina Marie de Camargo aged 16 makes her premiere at Paris Opera Ballets in "Les Caractères de la Danse"
Dutch 2nd Chamber passes child labor law
World championships of judo are 1st held in Tokyo
Pulitzer Prize for Music is awarded to Susie Ibarra her composition "Sky Islands"
Dayton teacherJohn T. Scopesarrested for teaching evolution in Tennessee
Lee Richmond, American baseball pitcher (first ever MLB perfect game 1880), born in Sheffield, Ohio (d. 1929)
Charles "Chief" Bender, American Baseball HOF pitcher (only indigenous American in BHOF; World Series 1910, 11, 13; no-hitter 1910; Philadelphia A's), born in Crow Wing County, Minnesota (d. 1954)
Agustín Barrios Mangoré, Paraguayan classical guitarist and composer (La Catedral), born in San Juan Bautista, Paraguay (d. 1944)
(Jacqueline) "Ilene" Woods, American radio singer and actress (Disney's Cinderella), born in Portsmouth, New Hampshire (d. 2010)
Miniature newspaper is published (Brighton Gazette 10 x 13 cm)
Hambletonian "daddy of 'em all", American top sire standardbred horse, born in Chester, New York (d. 1876)[1]
Netherlands and Denmark liberated from Nazi control
Rock and Roll Hall of Fame Foundation announces Cleveland, Ohio chosen as the site of Rock and Roll Hall of Fame Museum
George WashingtonappointsFriedrich Wilhelm von SteubenInspector General of the Continental Army
"West Side Story" soundtrack album goes #1 and stays #1 for 54 weeks, more than 20 weeks longer than any other album

Lucy Simon, American folk singer-songwriter (Simon Sisters), Grammy Award-winning record producer (In Harmony) and theater composer (The Secret Garden; Doctor Zhivago), born in New York City (d. 2022)[1]
Alexander Ragulin, Russian ice hockey player (Olympic gold 1964, 68, 72), born in Moscow (d. 2004)
Terry Baker, American College Football Hall of Fame quarterback (Heisman Trophy 1962, Oregon State), born in Pine River, Minnesota
Steve Stevens [Schneider], American rock guitarist and songwriter (Billy Idol - "Rebel Yell"; Bozzio Levin Stevens), born in New York City
Robert S. Abbott publishes 1st issue of newspaper "Chicago Defender"
The Kinks release "Waterloo Sunset" as a single; peaks at #2 on the British charts
Mary Kies is the first woman too be issued a US patent for weaving straw
City-wide fire burns for over 100 hours in Hamburg, Germany
French army intervenes in Puebla, Mexico: Cinco de Mayo
First US train robbery occurs at North Bend, Ohio
After sharing the Polo Grounds with the New York Giants for 10 years construction begins on Yankee Stadium in the Bronx, NYC
2 Fokker employees flee Nazi occupied Netherlands to England
Dutch Prime Minister Pieter Sjoerds Gerbrandy announces to the nation on Radio Orange that they are liberated
First squadron of jet aircraft boards a carrier
Broekster Boys soccer team forms in Damwoude
Eugene A. Marino installed as 1st African American Catholic archbishop, in Atlanta, Georgia
Iridium-1 Delta 2 Launch, Successful
Japan shuts down its nuclear reactors leaving the country without nuclear power for the first time since 1970
